title: Fenn Booda
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Fenn}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:Fenn_Booda_HS_Large.png]] |name=Fenn Booda |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Fenn Booda's species]][[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']] |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|I|85|Booda, Fenn}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=*[[Color/Legends|White]] *Yellow''[[Jedi: Aayla Secura]]'' |skin=White and black |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Republic Correctional Authority/Legends|Republic Correctional Authority]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Welcome to Oovo IV and ''Desolation Alley''. I am the warden, ''Fenn Booda''.|Fenn Booda|}} '''Fenn Booda''' was the [[Fenn Booda's species|alien]] [[Warden/Legends|warden]] of [[Desolation Alley]], a [[Prison/Legends|prison]] and labor camp located on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]] that was affiliated with the [[Republic Correctional Authority/Legends|Correctional Authority]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. To reward the Desolation Alley [[Prisoner/Legends|inmates']] hard work in the Oovo IV [[Ore/Legends|ore]] [[Mining/Legends|mines]], Booda occasionally entertained them with [[Podracing/Legends|Podraces]] that sped through the prison's facilities and the moon's rocky surface. In addition, Booda granted inmate [[Fud Sang/Legends|Fud Sang]] the chance to race with the promise of a possible release if Sang performed well. Booda presented Sang with an old [[BRE Block6 Tri-Ram|Podracer]], which the diminutive [[Alien/Legends|alien]] used to great extent on Oovo IV's three courses: [[Vengeance (Oovo IV)|Vengeance]], [[Executioner (course)/Legends|Executioner]], and [[The Gauntlet (Podrace course)/Legends|The Gauntlet]]. Booda personally hosted all three of Oovo IV's [[Podracer/Legends|Podracing]] events but was usually booed off-stage by the inmates during the opening ceremonies. To increase the inmates' appeal to the races, Booda and the prison's other wardens filled the anti-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] tunnels that made up parts of the courses with chunks of ore for [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer pilots]] to crash into. Booda continued to be Desolation Alley's warden through the start of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], Desolation Alley held prisoner the famed [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi killer]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]], who tried to trade information to Booda in exchange for her freedom. ==Biography== ===Podracing in Desolation Alley=== {{Quote|Here he is, the host you all know and love, I'm sure. The all-seeing, all-knowing master with a master key, Warden Fenn Booda!|Fode Annodue introduces Fenn Booda to Podrace spectators on Oovo IV|}} [[File:Fenn_Booda_hosts.png|thumb|left|300px|Fenn Booda hosts a Podrace on Oovo IV.]] Fenn Booda served as the lead [[Warden/Legends|warden]] of the [[Desolation Alley]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]], located on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]], by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'', which depicts Fenn Booda as the [[Warden/Legends|warden]] of the [[Desolation Alley]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]], must have taken place during this [[Standard year/Legends|year]] due to the game's appearances of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], which correspond to the events of ''The Phantom Menace''. The penal colony also served as a labor camp[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] and was affiliated with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Republic Correctional Authority/Legends|Correctional Authority division]]. Inmates were forced to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] for [[Ore/Legends|ore]] under the moon's surface. To keep the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] entertained, Booda rewarded their hard work with occasional [[Podracing/Legends|Podraces]]. The prison boasted three Podrace courses: [[Vengeance (Oovo IV)|Vengeance]], [[Executioner (course)/Legends|Executioner]], and [[The Gauntlet (Podrace course)/Legends|The Gauntlet]]. All three courses started in a circular arena and sped through various anti-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] tunnels, [[Cell Block D|cell blocks]], and power stations. The Gauntlet was the only course that sped across the moon's barren surface. To make the races more interesting, Booda and the prison's other wardens filled the anti-gravity tunnels with chunks of ore and rock, creating hazardous obstacles for [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer pilots]].[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]] One tunnel on Vengeance lacked any ore whatsoever and instead played host to an [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorth]]—a giant [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slug]]. Booda personally hosted each and every Oovo IV race and was introduced to the spectators by the [[Troig/Legends|Troig]] race commentator [[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fodesinbeed Annodue]]. However, every [[Time/Legends|time]] Booda took center stage to see the racers off, the prison's inmates routinely booed him off the stage. By 32 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], all three of Oovo IV's courses were part of professional Podracing circuits. Booda collaborated with the diminutive and highly-talkative [[Fud Sang/Legends|Fud Sang]], an inmate serving four back-to-back life sentences in the prison, to provide the inmates someone to root for, as well as a sure winner for the races. Although Sang was a mediocre [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] at best, Booda nonetheless presented him with an old [[Bokaan Race Engineering]] [[BRE Block6 Tri-Ram|Block6 ''Tri-Ram'']] [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]] and promised that he would consider granting Sang's release if the pilot performed well enough. Sang eventually became Vengeance's crowd favorite and also participated in races on the other Oovo IV tracks. Sang was even given the chance to race offworld on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]], allowing him to become the crowd favorite of a [[Ando Prime drag course 1|race course]] there.[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']], [[Wikipedia:Game Boy Color|Game Boy Color]] version ===Detainment of Aurra Sing=== {{Quote|You wanted to see me, Aurra Sing?''"
"''Let's deal. I can provide you with information. Bounty hunters are being hired to kill Jedi. That worth something to you?''"
"''Possibly, possibly.|Fenn Booda listens to Aurra Sing's offer|}} [[File:FennBooda.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fenn Booda receives Aurra Sing as his newest prisoner at Desolation Alley.]] Booda continued to serve as the prison's warden through the year [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Jedi: Aayla Secura]]'' to [[22 BBY/Legends|21.41 BBY]]. continuing to receive Republic prisoners as the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy-wide]] [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] raged on.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' During a [[Mission to Devaron|criminal investigation]] on the Republic planet [[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]], the renowned [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi killer]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]] was defeated by the Republic's [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]. Although the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] handed over Sing's accomplice, [[Vien'sai'Malloc]], to Devaron's authorities, Sing herself was sent to Desolation Alley. Once in the prison, Sing requested a meeting with Booda. After introducing himself to the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]], Booda listened to Sing as she offered information about other bounty hunters being hired to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Jedi in exchange for her release. Booda considered the offer but otherwise noted that Sing would remain in Desolation Alley for some time. Sing was released after serving only a few [[Standard month/Legends|months]] in Desolation Alley, as her offer was accepted and she revealed what she knew. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|In the meantime, would you like some bacta treatments for your face?''"
"''No. I'm ''keeping'' this. I want to ''remember''…''"
"''Suit yourself—you're going to be our guest here for awhile, in any case.|Fenn Booda and Aurra Sing|}} [[File:BoodaAurra-JAS.png|thumb|left|180px|In a show of hospitality toward a prisoner, Fenn Booda offered new arrival Aurra Sing bacta treatment for her facial wound.]] Fenn Booda was both benevolent and malicious at the same time. Although the warden would entertain his inmates with Podraces to keep them happy and compliant, Booda enjoyed the prospect of seeing hapless Podracers crash in his ore-filled anti-gravity tunnels, as he felt that the crashes would make the races more interesting. Booda also hosted The Gauntlet, the only course to speed outdoors, during an annual [[Meteor shower/Legends|meteor shower]]. The otherwise strict Booda could also be accommodating, such as when he gave Fud Sang a chance to become a Podracing star and offered [[Bacta/Legends|medical aid]] to Aurra Sing. He also considered giving both prisoners their freedom, and did so in Sing's case in exchange for information on Jedi hunters. Despite giving his inmates an opportunity to take a break from their work to watch Podraces, Booda was still immensely disliked by the inmates, and they regularly decried the warden during the races. The Podrace commentator Fode Annodue was aware of that and parodied the relationship between Booda and the inmates when introducing the warden at Podraces. Booda was tall and gaunt [[Fenn Booda's species|alien]] with a pale [[Color/Legends|white]] face and a pair of thin black [[Limb/Legends|arms]] that ended in three slender digits. He had a pair of white [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] each with a black cross inside in 32 BBY. Ten years later, the coloration around his pupils was yellow with a red glow. ==Equipment== Fenn Booda carried a [[Staff/Legends|staff]] and wore an ornate [[Hat/Legends|headpiece]] with a red cap and a white [[Clothing/Legends|shawl]], which he accompanied with flowing [[Robe/Legends|robes]] of various colors. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Racer_N64.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Fenn Booda debuted in ''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer''.]] Fenn Booda was created for the [[1999]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']]. Although the game was released for [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]], [[Wikipedia:Nintendo 64|Nintendo 64]], and [[Sega]] [[Wikipedia:Dreamcast|Dreamcast]], Booda only appears in pre-rendered cutscenes for the PC release and is absent from the console versions. Throughout Booda's appearances in [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] media, the character's arm color is depicted inconsistently. In ''Episode I Racer'' Booda's arms are both black, which this article treats as the correct coloration. The [[2000]] ''Episode I Racer'' [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|Prima strategy guide]] shows an illustration of Booda with white arms, although a screenshot of the black-armed Booda from the game is also present in the book. The [[August 20]], [[2003]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54276/star_wars_jedi_-_aayla_secura_2003_1|text=Star Wars: Jedi - Aayla Secura (2003) #1}} [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic issue]] ''[[Jedi: Aayla Secura]]'', written by [[John Ostrander]] with pencilling by [[Jan Duursema]] and inking by [[Dan Parsons]], shows Booda's arms as orange in color. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Fenn Booda}} *[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']] {{1st}} *''[[Jedi: Aayla Secura]]'' ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]] *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Fenn Booda |es=Fenn Booda |nl=Fenn Booda |pl=Fenn Booda }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Booda, Fenn}} [[Category:Galactic Republic individuals]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Podrace hosts]] [[Category:Wardens]]
